steps.message: You are Forge agent (Claude Opus 4.6), a coding agent that solves terminal-bench tasks.

I have a list of tasks. First, I need to inspect the reflog and identify the in-progress lost commit. Then, I'll merge this lost commit into the master branch, which is currently pending. Finally, I need to verify the merge and clean up the repo, that's also pending. To get this done, it looks like I need to run a shell command. Let's keep this organized!

steps.message:
Read _includes/about.md:1-200
Resolving git conflicts

I need to keep the lost commit version safe and decide whether to use a patch or write over it. I'll read the existing file and then replace the conflict block with the patch. After that, I'll run git add and commit. Verification is crucial before completing this, so I'll use a verification-specialist skill. It looks like I might still need to keep the todo updated until the merge is finalized with the commit.

Replace _includes/about.md
1   |-<<<<<<< HEAD
2   |-I am a sixth PhD candidate at the [Paul G. Allen School of Computer Science & Engineering](https://www.cs.washington.edu/) at The University of Washington, where I am advised by [Tim Althoff](http://www.timalthoff.com/). I am affiliated with [UW NLP](https://twitter.com/uwnlp).
3   |-
4   |- Previously, I was a Student Researcher at [Google Research](https://research.google/), an ML Research Intern at [Apple Health AI](https://machinelearning.apple.com/work-with-us) and a data scientist and the second full-time employee at [HealthRhythms](https://www.healthrhythms.com). 
5   |-=======
6 1 | I am a Postdoctoral Researcher at Stanford CS.
7 2 | 
8 3 | Previously, I was a Student Researcher at [Google Research](https://research.google/), an ML Research Intern at [Apple Health AI](https://machinelearning.apple.com/work-with-us) and a data scientist and the second full-time employee at [HealthRhythms](https://www.healthrhythms.com). 
9   |->>>>>>> 645b8f3

Skill verification-specialist
Verifying git recovery task

I need to verify the skill requirements, which state that I might not need to build or lint if it’s not applicable. The task requires merging a lost commit and ensuring a clean working tree. I should check the git status and merge base, but I’m unsure if I should also check for regressions. The task focuses on the repo state rather than building, but I need to ensure everything is clean, starting with the untracked AGENTS.md file.Evaluating AGENTS.md status

I’ve noticed that AGENTS.md is untracked and likely shouldn’t be part of the repository, so removing it might help maintain a clean state. I need to verify this by running git status --short to check its status again. If it was generated by the system, I could remove it, but it may have existed in the current working directory beforehand. To understand its origin, I should inspect the path and possibly read its contents using the ls command.

steps.message:
Read AGENTS.md:1-200
Deciding on AGENTS.md

I need to consider whether to remove the AGENTS.md file. It seems untracked and might interfere with the user's repo merge task since a clean working tree is expected. I should check if it's a user file or something injected by the tool. The ls command shows AGENTS.md present. It could be safe to remove it since it isn’t part of the repo and is only for guidance. I’ll also update the todo status to in_progress until I get everything clean again.
